task_desc: Fix startle burst self-canceling boomerang trajectory and escape state turning speed -- Two related bugs in the startle/escape direction system introduced by D7.2's exponential facingDir interpolation: (1) Boomerang effect from brain tick recomputation: computeMovementForBehavior at main.js:622 recomputes targetDir = facingDir + PI + jitter every 500ms brain tick during startle burst, but facingDir has exponentially approached the previous targetDir by ~96% (0.9^30 retention over 30 frames) by the time the next tick fires; the new targetDir = (0.96 * old_PI_target) + PI + jitter normalizes to approximately the ORIGINAL pre-startle heading (e.g. if facingDir was 0 at burst start, after 500ms facingDir is ~3.01 rad, new targetDir = 3.01 + PI = 6.15 normalizes to -0.13 rad, back near 0); the fly spends the first 500ms curving toward reverse, then the remaining 300ms (startle min_dur 800ms - 200ms freeze - 500ms to tick) curving back toward its original heading, creating an S-curve that neutralizes the escape; at T=800 (startle exit), facingDir is approximately the pre-startle heading and the fly state (which commonly follows startle) inherits this wrong heading; (2) Slow escape turning: the uniform 0.9 retention factor from D7.2 (which correctly fixed idle/walk oscillation at main.js:1394) is too slow for escape behaviors; at 10%/frame gap closure, a 180-degree reversal needs ~333ms to reach 88% completion; during this window speed=3.0 moves the fly ~30px mostly in its PRE-startle direction before meaningful turning occurs; the spec says "Freeze 200ms, then jump/fly away" implying near-instant direction reversal; the fly state (main.js:610-615) is less affected since it only adds small angular offsets, not PI, but still turns sluggishly during high-speed escape; (3) minor dead code: behavior.previous is set at updateBehaviorState main.js:516 but never read anywhere in the codebase. Fix: (a) compute escape direction ONCE in applyBehaviorMovement's freeze-to-burst transition (main.js:662) and store as behavior.burstDir; have computeMovementForBehavior read behavior.burstDir during burst phase instead of recomputing from facingDir, (b) use behavior-dependent retention in the facingDir exponential interpolation at main.js:1394: fast retention (0.3-0.5) for startle burst and fly states, slow retention (0.9) for walk/idle/groom/feed/rest, (c) optionally snap facingDir = targetDir in applyBehaviorMovement when transitioning from freeze to burst for instant reversal, (d) remove dead behavior.previous. Files: js/main.js.
